name: Potential Access Token Abuse
notes:
Converted from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Michaela Adams, Zach Mathis
SIGMA Title: Potential Access Token Abuse
SIGMA Description: Detects potential token impersonation and theft. Example, when using "DuplicateToken(Ex)" and "ImpersonateLoggedOnUser" with the "LOGON32_LOGON_NEW_CREDENTIALS flag".

False positives:
- Anti-Virus

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event matches devicetype=12 AND "Event ID"=4624 AND "Logon Type"=9 AND LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%advapi%' AND LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%negotiate%' AND LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%impersonationlevel%%%1833%' AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Potential Access Token Abuse on events which are detected by the LOCAL system

AND when the event matches devicetype=12 AND "Event ID"=4624 AND "Logon Type"=9 AND LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%advapi%' AND LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%negotiate%' AND LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%impersonationlevel%%%1833%' AQL filter query
RED_list: Event name: Potential Access Token Abuse
